report_name: DAEMON Tools Installers Compromised in Ongoing Supply-Chain Attack

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Actor
threat_actors.description: An unnamed threat actor responsible for the supply chain attack against DAEMON Tools. Kaspersky researchers noted the presence of Chinese-language artifacts in the malware implants, suggesting a possible origin.
threat_actors.goals: Information gathering and targeted espionage or data theft from high-value targets in specific industries.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The attackers compromised the official installers for DAEMON Tools, trojanizing them with malware. These installers were then distributed through the vendor's legitimate website.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The initial information-gathering implant downloads the second-stage QUIC RAT payload from a command-and-control server.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used a valid digital signature from the developer, AVB Disc Soft, to sign the trojanized installers, helping them evade detection. The second-stage payload also injects itself into legitimate processes like notepad.exe.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Command Shell
attack_patterns.description: The QUIC RAT implant is capable of executing arbitrary shell commands on the compromised system.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The first-stage malware collects system information (MAC address, hostname, processes, software, language) and exfiltrates it to the C2 server.

attack_patterns.name: System Information Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The initial payload acts as an information collector, gathering system details like MAC address, hostname, running processes, and installed software.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
attack_patterns.description: The malware achieves persistence by creating a startup entry, ensuring it runs every time the system boots.

attack_patterns.name: Process Injection
attack_patterns.description: The QUIC RAT implant was observed being injected into the legitimate system process notepad.exe to evade detection.

malware.name: Information Gatherer
malware.description: A first-stage payload bundled with trojanized DAEMON Tools installers. It collects system details and sends them to a C2 server, which then determines if a second-stage payload should be deployed.

malware.name: QUIC RAT
malware.description: A sophisticated second-stage backdoor selectively deployed to high-value targets. It is capable of downloading additional payloads, executing shell commands, and running shellcode in memory while injected into legitimate processes.

tools.name: DAEMON Tools
tools.description: A legitimate disk emulation utility whose official installers (versions 12.5.0.2421 to 12.5.0.2434) were trojanized in a supply chain attack.

tools.name: notepad.exe
tools.description: The legitimate Windows text editor process was used as a target for process injection by QUIC RAT to evade detection.

campaigns.name: DAEMON Tools Supply Chain Attack
campaigns.description: An ongoing supply chain attack, active since April 2026, where official DAEMON Tools installers were trojanized to distribute an information gatherer and a second-stage implant named QUIC RAT to targeted victims.

identities.name: AVB Disc Soft
identities.description: The developer of DAEMON Tools whose valid digital certificate was used to sign the trojanized installers distributed in the supply chain attack.

identities.name: Retail Organizations
identities.description: Organizations in the retail sector were among the high-value targets selected for the deployment of the second-stage QUIC RAT implant.

identities.name: Scientific Organizations
identities.description: Organizations in the scientific sector were among the high-value targets selected for the deployment of the second-stage QUIC RAT implant.

identities.name: Government Organizations
identities.description: Organizations in the government sector were among the high-value targets selected for the deployment of the second-stage QUIC RAT implant.

identities.name: Manufacturing Organizations
identities.description: Organizations in the manufacturing sector were among the high-value targets selected for the deployment of the second-stage QUIC RAT implant.

infrastructure.name: Typosquatted C2 Domain
infrastructure.description: A command-and-control server hosted on a typosquatted domain, registered a week before the attack, was used by the malware to receive commands and exfiltrate data.

indicators.name: Notepad Process with Outbound Network Connection
indicators.pattern:
title: Notepad Process with Outbound Network Connection
id: 1e8b9f0c-5a6b-4c7d-8e9f-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the notepad.exe process making an outbound network connection, which is highly unusual and could indicate process injection by malware like QUIC RAT.
references:
  - https://securelist.com/supply-chain-attack-via-daemon-tools/112630/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-05
tags:
  - attack.t1055
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\notepad.exe'
    Initiated: true
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the legitimate notepad.exe process making outbound network connections, an indicator of potential process injection as observed with QUIC RAT.

indicators.name: Trojanized DAEMON Tools Binaries
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Trojanized DAEMON Tools Binaries
id: 2d7c8e9f-4b5a-4d6c-9e8f-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: Detects file creation events for DAEMON Tools binaries known to be compromised in a supply chain attack.
references:
  - https://securelist.com/supply-chain-attack-via-daemon-tools/112630/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-05
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\DTHelper.exe'
      - '\DiscSoftBusServiceLite.exe'
      - '\DTShellHlp.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects filenames associated with the trojanized DAEMON Tools installers.
